mapping.name: Improper Credential Usage
mapping.description: Covers hardcoded credentials, insecure credential storage, and improper handling of authentication tokens in mobile applications. Attackers can extract credentials through reverse engineering, device backups, or filesystem access.

mapping.name: Inadequate Supply Chain Security
mapping.description: Addresses risks from third-party libraries, SDKs, and components integrated into mobile applications without proper vetting, integrity verification, or version pinning. Compromised dependencies can introduce malware, data exfiltration, or backdoors.

mapping.name: Insecure Authentication/Authorization
mapping.description: Covers weaknesses in authentication mechanisms including biometric implementation flaws, missing multi-factor authentication, and client-side authorization checks that can be bypassed.

mapping.name: Insufficient Input/Output Validation
mapping.description: Addresses lack of input validation on data received through deep links, IPC mechanisms, clipboard, and other input channels. Insufficient validation enables injection attacks, path traversal, and unintended application behavior.

mapping.name: Insecure Communication
mapping.description: Covers network communication vulnerabilities including missing certificate pinning, disabled TLS verification, cleartext traffic, and improper hostname validation. These flaws enable man-in-the-middle attacks and traffic interception.

mapping.name: Inadequate Privacy Controls
mapping.description: Addresses excessive collection of personal data, device identifiers, and location information without user consent or legitimate purpose. Includes lack of data minimization, missing consent mechanisms, and non-compliance with privacy regulations.

mapping.name: Insufficient Binary Protections
mapping.description: Covers absence of code obfuscation, anti-tampering mechanisms, root/jailbreak detection, and debugger detection. Without these protections, attackers can reverse-engineer the application, understand its logic, and create modified versions.

mapping.name: Security Misconfiguration
mapping.description: Addresses insecure default configurations including debug mode in production, enabled backups, cleartext traffic permissions, exported components without protection, and overly permissive file permissions.

mapping.name: Insecure Data Storage
mapping.description: Covers storage of sensitive data in unprotected locations including external storage, unencrypted databases, cache directories, log files, and clipboard. Data stored insecurely can be accessed by other applications or extracted from the device.

mapping.name: Insufficient Cryptography
mapping.description: Addresses use of weak, broken, or deprecated cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, ECB mode), hardcoded encryption keys, insufficient key lengths, and improper implementation of cryptographic operations.
